idea.topic: Voyager vs. Light to Oort Cloud
idea.hook: Think leaving our Solar System is fast?
facts: The Oort Cloud, the true edge of our Sun's gravitational influence, starts around 2,000 Astronomical Units (AU) away.
Voyager 1, our fastest spacecraft, travels at ~17 kilometers per second (38,000 mph).
At Voyager 1's speed, reaching the inner Oort Cloud would take approximately 560 years.
At the speed of light (~300,000 km/s), the journey would take only about 11.6 days.
idea.payoff: Even our closest cosmic neighborhood is unimaginably vast, making interstellar travel with current technology a centuries-long endeavor compared to light speed's mere days.
idea.title: Leave Our Solar System? NOT FAST! 🤯🚀

scenes.text: Think leaving our Solar System is fast?
scenes.visual: A visual zoom-out from Earth, showing planets orbiting the Sun, then shrinking to a distant star.

scenes.text: Oort Cloud: true edge of Sun's influence. Starts ~2,000 AU away.
scenes.visual: An animated diagram showing the Solar System as a tiny dot in the center of a vast, transparent sphere representing the Oort Cloud. Text '2,000 AU' appears.

scenes.text: Voyager 1: 17 km/s. To Oort Cloud: ~560 years.
scenes.visual: A stylized Voyager 1 spacecraft icon slowly moving from left to right across the screen, leaving a long, faint trail. A timer counts up towards '560 Years'.

scenes.text: Light speed: 300,000 km/s. To Oort Cloud: ~11.6 days!
scenes.visual: A vibrant beam of light streaks across the screen from left to right in a flash, quickly reaching the same distance. A timer quickly counts up to '11.6 Days'.

scenes.text: Interstellar travel: centuries vs. days. Space is unimaginably vast!
scenes.visual: A stunning, deep space visual of distant galaxies and nebulae, emphasizing infinite scale and beauty. The text pulsates for emphasis.

youtube.title: Leave Our Solar System? NOT FAST! 🤯🚀
